channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Isolated Chicago communities secure money for a coveted transit project before Trump takes office
item.description: Decades after a former Chicago mayor pledged to extend transit to the city’s far South Side, the federal government is committing to the massive project just before President Joe Biden leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Mcmurray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What Trump's decision to wade into spending fight tells us about the next 4 years
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After days of threats and demands, Donald Trump had little to show for it once lawmakers passed a budget deal in the early hours of Saturday, narrowly averting a pre-Christmas government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, STEPHEN GROVES, JILL COLVIN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers react to stopgap funding and averting government shutdown
item.description: Lawmakers are having their say after President Biden signed a stopgap bill into law on Saturday, extending government funding into March and avoiding a shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shutdown saga exposes new fault lines for Speaker Johnson and tests his grip on the gavel
item.description: One of the most turbulent sessions of Congress in the modern era is about to make way for the next one.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: President Biden signs stopgap funding bill into law, narrowly averting shutdown
item.description: The White House has announced that President Biden signed a stopgap funding bill into law on Saturday, extending government funding into March and avoiding a shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate review of Supreme Court ethics finds more luxury trips and urges enforceable code of conduct
item.description: A nearly two-year investigation by Democratic senators of Supreme Court ethics details more luxury travel by Justice Clarence Thomas and urges Congress to establish a way to enforce a new code of conduct.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Were undercover sources from other DOJ agencies present on Jan. 6? Grassley, Johnson demand answers
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: Senate Republicans demand answers on whether confidential human sources from DOJ agencies beyond the FBI were used on Jan. 6, 2021, while also questioning IG Horowitz's review of records.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brief government shutdown ushered in before Christmas as Senate works to advance House bill
item.description: Congress did not manage to send a spending bill to President Biden's desk before the pre-Christmas deadline.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nevada election leaders find nearly 200 cases of double votes from November
item.description: The Nevada Secretary of State’s Office is investigating 180 double votes from the 2024 general election, a report released Friday indicated.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Valadao, Fong vote for government funding bill to avoid shutdown
item.description: Reps. David Valadao and Vince Fong both voted for a government funding bill that passed the House late Friday afternoon just hours before a shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US says it killed ISIS leader in Syria in 'targeted' airstrike
item.description: The U.S. military killed ISIS leader Abu Yusif and another member in an airstrike conducted in Syria on Thursday, U.S. Central Command said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump adds Europe to the list of US trade partners he's threatening with tariffs
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday added the 27 countries that make up the European Union to the list of trade partners he's threatening with tariffs — unless the group takes steps to import more U.S. goods.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota House tie in peril after judge rules DFL lawmaker can’t be sworn in
item.description: A district court judge on Friday granted a challenge to Democratic Rep.-elect Curtis Johnson’s residency, blocking him from taking office.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: A day before a potential government shutdown, the House resoundingly rejected President-elect Donald Trump's new plan to fund operations and suspend the debt ceiling, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Government funding bill clears Congress and heads to President Biden, averting a shutdown
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown. It now goes to President Joe Biden, who is expected to quickly sign it into law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: House rejects Johnson's plan B to prevent shutdown
item.description: The House on Thursday torpedoed Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan B to avert a government shutdown and suspend the debt limit, dealing a blow to the GOP leader and catapulting the conference back to square one as it stares down Friday's funding deadline.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dems say 'hell no' to GOP plan B on government funding, debt ceiling
item.description: House Democrats are rallying in overwhelming opposition to the Republicans' second stab at a government funding bill, vowing to sink it on the House floor and force GOP leaders back to the table to negotiate a bipartisan compromise.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP eyes plan B stopgap with separate 2-part debt limit commitment
item.description: House Republicans are eyeing an alternative government funding stopgap that excludes a debt ceiling increase, and are instead working on a commitment to raise the borrowing limit twice next year in a bid to appease President-elect Trump, two sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Former top aide to Mayor Adams surrenders to DA's office to face charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams surrendered to authorities Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to the administration of a mayor who has seen who is facing his own indictment and investigations into multiple aides and allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: House Ethics Committee secretly voted to release Matt Gaetz ethics report, source says
item.description: The House Ethics Committee has voted in secret to release the long-awaited ethics report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump, Vance call for clean CR, debt ceiling hike, urge GOP to sink Johnson compromise
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect Vance on Wednesday called for Republicans to approve a clean stopgap funding bill paired with a hike to the debt ceiling, saying the previous government-funding measure negotiated by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) should be torpedoed.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: In last-ditch effort, Democrats push to get Julie Su confirmed as labor secretary
item.description: Acting Secretary Julie Su has led the Labor Department for nearly two years, despite never getting a Senate confirmation vote. With time running out, her staunchest supporters haven't given up.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Nevada's 6 Electoral College votes certified for President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Nevada Republican Party Chairman Michael McDonald officially certified the state's six Electoral College votes for President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday during a gathering on the UNR campus in Reno.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In disputed Shakopee-area legislative race, voters share what was on uncounted ballots
item.description: While 20 ballots believed to have been discarded before being counted won’t be recovered, some voters who cast those missing ballots shared what was on them. They appear to show Rep. Brad Tabke’s narrow lead would stand up.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York judge says Trump is not immune from hush money conviction
item.description: A spokesperson for President-elect Trump said the case should never have been brought, called for its immediate dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Judge rejects Trump’s bid to toss hush money conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge Monday refused to throw out President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction because of the U.S. Supreme Court's recent ruling on presidential immunity. But the overall future of the historic case remains unclear. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: TikTok asks the Supreme Court for an emergency order to block a U.S. ban unless it’s sold
item.description: A similar plea was filed by content creators who rely on the platform for income and some of TikTok’s more than 170 million users in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden establishes a national monument for Frances Perkins, the 1st female Cabinet secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday established a national monument honoring the late FDR-era Labor Secretary Frances Perkins, the first woman to serve in a presidential Cabinet and a driving force behind Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal programs after the Great Depression.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5
